findings.evidence: BaseChange.lean:34-37 has [Semiring A] only; Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Basic.lean:435 (instCommSemiring) requires [CommSemiring A] [CommSemiring B]; Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:163 (instIsCocomm) requires [IsCocomm S A] [IsCocomm R B].
findings.fix: Remove or qualify the sentence: either drop it, or note that these instances become available under the additional commutativity/cocommutativity hypotheses.
findings.issue: Module docstring claims commutativity and cocommutativity of the base change are 'already supplied' by Mathlib instances, but the file assumes only [Semiring A] and [HopfAlgebra k A]; the cited instances require [CommSemiring A] and [IsCocomm k A] respectively.

findings.evidence: BaseChange.lean:36 has [HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B]; the docstring at line 49 says 'from k-bialgebras'; Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map and BialgHom.id only need Bialgebra but the binder demands HopfAlgebra.
findings.fix: Change 'k-bialgebras' to 'k-Hopf algebras' in the docstring, or relax the variable binder to [Bialgebra k A] if the proof only needs bialgebra structure.
findings.issue: The baseChangeMap docstring says 'the base-change functor from k-bialgebras to K-bialgebras', but the definition requires [HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B]; a user with only [Bialgebra k A] cannot call it.

summary: Two docstrings overclaim what the code actually delivers: the module docstring asserts commutativity/cocommutativity instances that require stronger hypotheses than the file provides, and the baseChangeMap docstring claims a functor defined on bialgebras when the binder requires the stronger HopfAlgebra.
